methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Neuromeka is a public robotics and manufacturing company listed on KOSDAQ (ticker 277810.KQ) that has raised $36.5M to date. The valuation exercise draws from a fragmented peer universe comprising one public comparable and eight private competitors in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method is inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. The sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints—the threshold required for reliable median-based triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias and lack specificity to Neuromeka's competitive position.

Given the data constraints, a traditional three-method triangulation cannot be completed. Valuation support would require either (1) deployment of alternative methods such as revenue multiple analysis or DCF sensitivity modeling against the fragmented private peer set, or (2) direct reliance on the single public comparable at 277810.KQ with explicit disclosure of methodological limitations. Key uncertainties center on the representativeness of the private peer cohort, stage heterogeneity within the broader Robotics & Manufacturing universe, and the applicability of Korean public market pricing to Neuromeka's business profile. Further data collection on revenue, EBITDA, or growth rates among identified peers is necessary to support a quantitative range.
